title: Add env multi-select filter to dashboard
description:
## Context
Per SPEC.md's Phase 1 filter requirement: date range + env multi-select. The days
filter exists via ?days=7|30|90; env filter does not. With three writers soon
(clear-pipeline prod, staging, Nikita's local-nikita), each person needs to
focus on their own rows.

## Files to touch
- [src/lib/queries.ts](src/lib/queries.ts) — every aggregate function takes an
  optional `envs: string[]` param and appends `AND pr.env = ANY(${envs})` (or the
  llm_call-side equivalent) when non-empty. Add `fetchAvailableEnvs()` returning
  all distinct `pipeline_run.env` values.
- [src/app/page.tsx](src/app/page.tsx) — read `searchParams.env` (may be string |
  string[]), normalise to string[], pass to each query. Render a horizontal list
  of checkbox-links: each toggles one env in/out of the URL.

## UI sketch
```
Filter: [✓] prod  [✓] staging  [ ] local-nikita  [clear]
```
Each checkbox is an `<a>` that links to the same page with the env list updated
(add if not present, remove if present). Server-rendered, no JS.

## Testing
- Local smoke: insert runs across envs, confirm filters narrow each section.
- Edge case: selected env with zero calls in the window — each section shows
  its "No data"/"No runs" state.
- `npm run build` must pass.

## Out of scope
- Stage filter (future — start with env since it's the team's primary axis).
- Date-range picker beyond the existing 7d/30d/90d links.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard URL accepts repeated ?env= query params (e.g. ?env=prod&env=staging). All five aggregates (hero, by-env chart, by-stage chart, top runs, models seen) are filtered. UI shows checkboxes for each distinct env discovered in the DB. Clicking a checkbox navigates to a new URL (server-side filtering, no client state). Empty selection = all envs (current behaviour).

comments.author: positonic
comments.text: Implemented in d785841. Added fetchAvailableEnvs() (DISTINCT pr.env). Every aggregate fn gains optional envs[] and joins pipeline_run; applied to hero, by-env, by-stage, top-runs, models-seen AND to the parse-error/latency/cache tiles added by 2id/7xm/82h so filtering is consistent across the whole page. Days nav links preserve env params. Env chip strip is server-rendered <a> toggles with a 'clear' link. NOTE: dropped `= ANY(${envs})` in favour of `IN (${sql.join(...)})` because drizzle's sql template spreads JS arrays into separate parameters, so the ANY-array form errored with 'malformed array literal' when postgres-js flattened the array to the first element. Verified: ?env=prod narrows to claude-sonnet-4-6 only; ?env=local-nikita narrows to nikita-v1; ?env=prod&env=staging combines both; unknown env yields empty states; no env param = all envs unchanged. lint + build pass.

title: call_rating table: schema + migration
description:
Add call_rating table to src/db/schema.ts and generate a SQL migration.

Schema:
- id uuid PK default gen_random_uuid()
- call_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES llm_call(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
- rater text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'james' (plain string until auth lands)
- verdict text NOT NULL — enforce allowed values in API layer, not DB, so it stays easy to extend. Allowed: correct | wrong_group | should_be_new | should_have_merged | unclear
- confidence smallint NULL (1-5)
- notes text NULL
- created_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
- UNIQUE (call_id, rater)

Index: (created_at DESC) for newest-first listing joins.

Run: npm run db:generate to produce migrations/0002_*.sql. Do NOT run db:push — just generate the SQL.

title: Add parse-error-rate tile to dashboard
description:
## Context
The `llm_call.parse_error` column is already populated by `POST /api/calls` whenever
the pipeline fails to parse Claude's response, but nothing surfaces it in the UI.
This is the fastest signal that a prompt is drifting — a spike means a stage is
producing output that no longer parses. Should be a prominent tile next to the
24h hero stat so it's the first thing the team sees on a bad day.

## Files to touch
- [src/lib/queries.ts](src/lib/queries.ts) — add `fetchParseErrorRate(days)` that
  returns `{stage, errors, total, rate}[]` grouped by stage. Include only rows where
  `created_at >= now() - $days * interval '1 day'`.
- [src/app/page.tsx](src/app/page.tsx) — render a new section between the hero tile
  and the stacked-bar grid. One row per stage with: stage name, error count /
  total count, percentage. Red text when rate > 1%. Grey '—' row when no calls.

## SQL sketch
```sql
SELECT
  stage,
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE parse_error IS NOT NULL) AS errors,
  COUNT(*) AS total
FROM llm_call
WHERE created_at >= now() - ($1::int * interval '1 day')
GROUP BY stage
ORDER BY stage;
```

## Testing
- Insert a few rows locally via `docker exec insights-pg psql ...` with
  `parse_error` set and unset, refresh http://localhost:3001, confirm percentages
  match.
- `npm run build` must pass.

## Out of scope
- Historical chart of error rate over time (future phase).
- Drill-down to individual failing calls (Phase 3 call browser).
acceptance_criteria: A new tile on / shows the parse error rate for the selected window (count(parse_error IS NOT NULL) / count(*)) broken down by stage. Shows '—' when no calls in range. Visible screenshots of the tile with and without errors.

comments.author: positonic
comments.text: Implemented in eefd35c. Added fetchParseErrorRate() in src/lib/queries.ts and a table section on src/app/page.tsx (between hero and stacked-bar grid) showing errors/total/rate per stage. Red when rate > 1%. Verified locally with seeded rows: classify 3/4=75% rendered red; assess/group 0% not red. Empty state shows '—'. lint + build pass.

title: Row + verdict widget client component
description:
Add src/app/review/group/row.tsx (client component) — the interactive rating widget inside each list row.

Visible at rest (per row):
- Signal line: signal_id + whatever title/description we can pull from parsed_response.signal fields OR a truncated user_prompt. Don't over-engineer parsing yet — truncate user_prompt to 240 chars if no better source.
- Decision line: parsed_response.action + target event_id + rewritten title. If parse_error, show it in red.
- Verdict buttons: 5 buttons — correct / wrong_group / should_be_new / should_have_merged / unclear. Current verdict highlighted.
- Notes: textarea, 2 rows, saves on blur.
- Meta: prompt_version, env, relative time (e.g. '12m ago').

Interactions:
- Click a verdict button → POST to /api/ratings with the bearer token. Show optimistic state; revert on error.
- Hotkeys when row is focused: 1-5 maps to the 5 verdicts. Ship this in v1 — it's the whole point of fast rating.
- Focus moves to next unrated row after successful rating.

Do NOT add auth — rater is hardcoded 'james' until Phase 3b.

The bearer token is server-side only. So the POST needs to go through a Next.js server action, not a raw fetch from the browser. Use a server action exported from page.tsx (or a neighbouring actions.ts) and call it from the client component.

title: Add p50/p95 latency by stage to dashboard
description:
## Context
`llm_call.latency_ms` is captured per call but never shown. p50/p95 per stage is
the standard operational metric for spotting model regressions, Anthropic
slowdowns, or prompt bloat. Pairs well with the parse-error-rate tile.

## Files to touch
- [src/lib/queries.ts](src/lib/queries.ts) — add `fetchLatencyByStage(days)`
  returning `{stage, calls, p50, p95, p99}[]`.
- [src/app/page.tsx](src/app/page.tsx) — new table section below the stacked
  bar charts, before "Top 20 runs". Columns: STAGE, CALLS, p50 ms, p95 ms, p99 ms.
  Red when p95 > 5000ms.

## SQL sketch
```sql
SELECT
  stage,
  COUNT(*) AS calls,
  percentile_cont(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY latency_ms)::int AS p50,
  percentile_cont(0.95) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY latency_ms)::int AS p95,
  percentile_cont(0.99) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY latency_ms)::int AS p99
FROM llm_call
WHERE latency_ms IS NOT NULL
  AND created_at >= now() - ($1::int * interval '1 day')
GROUP BY stage
ORDER BY stage;
```

## Testing
- Insert ~20 rows per stage locally with varying `latency_ms` values, confirm the
  percentiles match hand-checked values.
- `npm run build` must pass.

## Out of scope
- Latency histograms (future).
- Latency by model (future — current signal is stage-level).
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard shows a table with one row per stage: calls, p50_ms, p95_ms, p99_ms. Values formatted with thousand separators. Percentile computation uses PostgreSQL's percentile_cont aggregate. Verified with seeded rows.

comments.author: positonic
comments.text: Implemented in c9287bc. Added fetchLatencyByStage() in src/lib/queries.ts using percentile_cont(0.5/0.95/0.99). New table section on src/app/page.tsx between the bar grid and top-runs. Red p95 when > 5000ms. Verified with 20 seeded rows per stage: classify p50=1110 p95=1200 p99=1714 (hand-checked matches linear interpolation), group p95=6950 rendered red. Values comma-formatted via toLocaleString. lint + build pass.

title: Add cache hit rate + cache savings tile
description:
## Context
Prompt caching is a big cost lever for the pipeline (system prompts are large and
repeated). We already store `cache_read_tokens` and `cache_create_tokens` but
surface nothing. A hit ratio tile tells the team whether caching is working, and
a "dollars saved" estimate makes the impact legible.

## Files to touch
- [src/lib/queries.ts](src/lib/queries.ts) — add `fetchCacheStats(days)`
  returning `{model, input, cache_read, cache_create}[]` grouped by model.
- [src/app/page.tsx](src/app/page.tsx) — new tile next to the hero stat showing
  hit ratio (%) and estimated savings (USD). Compute savings server-side using
  [src/lib/prices.ts](src/lib/prices.ts) — for each priced model, savings =
  cache_read_tokens * (full_input_price - cache_read_price) / 1e6. Skip models
  missing from MODEL_PRICES.

## SQL sketch
```sql
SELECT
  model,
  SUM(input_tokens) AS input,
  SUM(cache_read_tokens) AS cache_read,
  SUM(cache_create_tokens) AS cache_create
FROM llm_call
WHERE created_at >= now() - ($1::int * interval '1 day')
GROUP BY model;
```

## Testing
- Insert rows locally with and without cache_read populated; confirm ratio and
  savings math.
- `npm run build` must pass.

## Out of scope
- Cache hit rate trend over time.
- Per-prompt cache analysis.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard shows a 'Prompt caching' tile with: cache hit ratio (cache_read / (input_tokens + cache_read)), total cache_read_tokens, total cache_create_tokens, estimated dollars saved by caching (cache_read_tokens * model's full input price - cache_read_tokens * model's cacheRead price, summed across models present in MODEL_PRICES). Null-model rows excluded from savings calc.

comments.author: positonic
comments.text: Implemented in 1134137. fetchCacheStats() groups SUMs by model; summarizeCache() on the page computes hit ratio and savings using MODEL_PRICES (models missing from the map are excluded from the savings calc but still counted in total read/create). New tile sits beside the 24h hero in a 2-col grid. Verified with 50000 cache_read on claude-sonnet-4-6 + existing 100900 input: hit=33.1%, savings=$0.135 (hand-check: 50000*(3-0.3)/1e6=0.135). lint + build pass.

title: /review/group page: server query + layout
description:
Add src/app/review/group/page.tsx as a server component.

Query: fetch llm_call rows WHERE stage='group', joined to pipeline_run (for env) and LEFT JOINed to call_rating (for current verdict). Default sort: llm_call.created_at DESC. Default limit: 50 (pagination can wait).

Filters via URL searchParams:
- env[] (multi-select — reuse StackedBar env pattern from existing dashboard)
- from, to (date range, default last 7d)
- verdict: 'unrated' | 'any' (default 'unrated' to focus review work)
- prompt_version (optional single value)

Layout (Tailwind):
- Filter bar at top — stripped-down copy of existing dashboard filter UI
- List of expandable rows (see child task for row component)
- Count + 'showing N of M' at top

Each row needs to render compactly; expanded state shows the raw system_prompt + user_prompt + raw_response. Use <details> initially to avoid client-component overhead.

title: POST /api/ratings endpoint
description:
Add src/app/api/ratings/route.ts.

Behavior:
- POST only. Reject other methods.
- Auth: require Authorization: Bearer $INSIGHTS_INGEST_TOKEN (reuse auth helper in src/lib/auth.ts — same pattern as /api/calls).
- Body: { call_id: uuid, rater?: string, verdict: string, confidence?: 1-5, notes?: string }
- Validate verdict ∈ ['correct','wrong_group','should_be_new','should_have_merged','unclear'] — reject with 400 otherwise.
- Upsert on (call_id, rater): if row exists, update verdict/confidence/notes/created_at. This lets the reviewer change their mind by re-submitting.
- Return 200 with { id }.

No need for a GET — the /review/group page queries Drizzle directly (server component).
close_reason: Replaced with a Next.js server action inside /review/group. The bearer token is server-side only, so the rating UI posts via a server action directly into Drizzle — no HTTP endpoint needed. An external POST /api/ratings can be added later if a script or external system needs to write ratings; YAGNI for now.

title: Phase 3b: Cluster-level event review UI
description:
Build /review/events page backed by clear-api GraphQL. Import/Clear UX: reviewer imports N recent events + their signals into local mirror tables, rates each event as a whole, clears imports when done. Ratings survive clears (keyed on event_id).

Composes with Phase 3a: event detail view drills into the stage='group' llm_call rows for that event_id and shows both the event-level verdict and the per-call verdicts side by side.

title: Import + Clear server actions, /review/events listing
description:
Add src/app/review/events/page.tsx + actions.ts.

actions.ts:
- importEvents({ sinceIso, limit }): calls clearApi.fetchRecentEvents, upserts imported_event and imported_signal (ON CONFLICT id DO UPDATE). Returns { eventsImported: N, signalsImported: M }.
- clearImports(): DELETE FROM imported_event (cascades to imported_signal). Does NOT touch event_rating. Returns { cleared: N }.
- rateEvent({ eventId, verdict, confidence?, notes? }): upsert on (event_id, rater). Verdict must be in EVENT_VERDICTS.

page.tsx (server component):
- Buttons: 'Import last 7d', 'Import last 30d', 'Clear imports'
- Table of imported_event rows: title, types, rank, valid_from, member signal count, current verdict (LEFT JOIN event_rating on event_id AND rater='james')
- Row click expands to show: description, list of member signals (title, source, publishedAt), verdict widget, notes
- Counts at top: 'X events imported · Y rated · Z unrated'

Defer event-to-call drill-in to the next bead.

title: Event detail: drill-in to stage='group' llm_calls
description:
When a reviewer expands an imported event, show all stage='group' llm_call rows where event_id = this_event.id alongside the member signals. Join to call_rating so the reviewer sees both the event-level verdict and the per-call verdicts on one page.

Use this to surface conflicts: event rated 'coherent' but 2 of its group calls rated 'wrong_group' → highlight in yellow. This is the 'loops compose' moment from the strategy doc.

title: Ops: provision clear-api service user + API key for insights
description:
Before Phase 3b can hit clear-api, provision a dedicated service user in clear-api with an API key.

DECIDED 2026-04-24: service user gets role='admin' (not team-scoped). Reason: clear-api's teamId filter on events resolves via location coverage; a team-scoped user would see only events in that team's geography, a subset of what the pipeline produced. Engineers reviewing pipeline quality need the full pipeline output.

Steps (manual, for James):
1. Create a dedicated user account in clear-api — e.g. 'insights-service' — and grant role='admin'.
2. Log in as that user; run the createApiKey GraphQL mutation to generate an sk_live_* token.
3. Drop into this repo's .env.local:
     CLEAR_API_URL=http://localhost:4000/graphql  (or staging URL)
     CLEAR_API_TOKEN=sk_live_...
     CLEAR_API_TEAM_ID=   (leave blank — admin means no teamId needed)
4. Add the same three to Railway env vars for the deployed insights app.

Phase 3b code can ship without this, but Import will return 401 until it's done.
close_reason: James confirmed key is created and saved in .env.local (2026-04-24). URL target: https://api.clearinitiative.io/graphql.

title: Spike: decide token strategy (one shared vs per-writer)
description:
## Context
Currently one shared `INSIGHTS_INGEST_TOKEN` authenticates every POST. This is
fine for 1–2 trusted writers. As soon as Nikita's classifier joins, three people
know the token (James, Prajava, Nikita). At four or five writers, a leak forces
rotating every consumer.

Options:

- **(A) Keep shared token.** Simplest. Rotation = update Vercel + DM new value
  to each writer. Any leak = rotate everyone.
- **(B) Per-writer tokens server-side.** `INSIGHTS_INGEST_TOKENS` becomes a
  JSON map like `{"clear-pipeline-prod":"hex...", "nikita":"hex..."}`. Auth
  middleware looks up the token and stores the matched writer name in a
  (new, optional) `llm_call.written_by` column. Leak = rotate just one.

## Tradeoffs
- B is a half-day change (add a column, tweak auth, teach writers to use their
  own token).
- B gives post-hoc attribution for noisy writers.
- A is still defensible if the team stays small.

## What this spike produces
A decision entry in [SPEC.md](SPEC.md)'s Decisions section, plus either:
- If A: close this ticket, no code.
- If B: open a follow-up implementation ticket with the column addition +
  auth.ts change.

## Research steps
1. Confirm how many writers are realistic in the next 3 months (ask James).
2. Look at whether Vercel env vars can hold a JSON map cleanly (they can — it's
   just a string).
3. Consider if attribution (knowing who wrote which row) has value beyond auth.

## Out of scope
- Implementing option B in this ticket — that's a separate ticket if chosen.
- OAuth, API keys per user, signed requests — all overkill for Phase 1.
acceptance_criteria: A short written recommendation (added to SPEC.md under 'Decisions') picking one of: (A) keep one shared INSIGHTS_INGEST_TOKEN, (B) move to a map of named tokens server-side. Covers: rotation cost, leak blast radius, how many writers trigger the switch, whether to log which token ingested each row. No code required unless the decision is 'switch now'.

comments.author: positonic
comments.text:
Draft recommendation (James to review + paste into SPEC.md Decisions when you're ready).

**Recommendation: (A) Keep one shared INSIGHTS_INGEST_TOKEN for Phase 1.**

Reasoning:
- Writer count on the 3-month horizon is 3: clear-pipeline prod, clear-pipeline staging, Nikita's classifier. All three hosts are trusted operators on the team.
- Rotation cost at 3 writers: one Vercel env update + three DMs. 10 min. Not painful enough to pay the (B) complexity tax up front.
- Leak blast radius is bounded to writing bogus llm_call rows. There is no PII in the table and no production system depends on these rows being correct. Worst case we truncate the table and keep going. This is **not** worth the attribution mechanism on its own.
- Attribution value is low *for auth purposes* but there is a real monitoring case: the 'models seen' table already makes each writer visible by the model string they choose (claude-sonnet-4-6 vs nikita-v1), and pipeline_run.env already separates prod/staging/local-nikita. So we already have post-hoc attribution at row granularity — a written_by column would duplicate info already carried by (model, env).

**Trigger to revisit (flip to B):** any of
1. Writer count hits 5 (one more pipeline or a second researcher). Rotation overhead becomes real.
2. First observed misattribution (someone writing rows with the wrong env/model). A per-writer token gives a provenance check the values can't be faked past.
3. We add any non-public data to the schema (e.g. customer identifiers in signal_id). Leak radius changes.

**Logging which token ingested each row:** not now. If we move to (B), add the column at that time — adding it before that point is speculative schema change.

No code needed for this ticket. If you approve, update SPEC.md Decisions section and close. If you pick (B) instead, open a follow-up ticket covering: INSIGHTS_INGEST_TOKENS JSON parsing in src/lib/auth.ts + optional written_by text column (nullable, additive).

title: clear-api: propose pagination + date filters on events/signals queries
description:
Cross-repo ask for the clear-api team.

Current state (as of 2026-04-24): events(teamId: String) and signals(teamId: String) return ALL matching rows with no limit/offset/cursor and no date filter.

For clear-pipeline-insights Phase 3b import UX to scale past small teams, we need one of:
- events(teamId, validFromAfter: DateTime, validFromBefore: DateTime, first: Int): [Event!]!
- Relay-style: events(teamId, first: Int, after: String, validFromAfter: DateTime): EventConnection

Bring to clear-api team. Not blocking Phase 3b MVP (we can filter client-side for demo-size data) but will block at scale.

title: Mirror schema: imported_event, imported_signal, event_rating
description:
Add three tables to src/db/schema.ts.

imported_event (id=text PK, clear-api cuid; fields mirror clear-api GraphQL Event type: title, description, types text[], rank real, valid_from/valid_to, first_signal_created_at/last_signal_created_at, population_affected text, origin_location_id, destination_location_id, location_id; plus raw_event jsonb for future-proofing, imported_at, team_id).

imported_signal (id=text PK, event_id text NOT NULL REFERENCES imported_event(id) ON DELETE CASCADE; source_id, source_name, title, description, url, published_at, collected_at, raw_signal jsonb, imported_at). Index on event_id.

event_rating (id uuid PK, event_id text NOT NULL, rater text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'james', verdict text NOT NULL, confidence smallint, notes text, created_at timestamptz default now(), UNIQUE (event_id, rater)). Index on event_id.

IMPORTANT: event_rating.event_id is NOT a FK to imported_event. Deliberate: clearing imports must not cascade into rating deletes. On re-import, rebinding happens via JOIN.

Verdict is FREE-TEXT, NO enum, NO CHECK constraint. Deliberate: per strategy doc, let the vocabulary emerge from real ratings before formalizing. Phase 3c will tighten into an enum once we see what the team actually types.

Run: npm run db:generate. Commit the generated migrations/0002_*.sql.

title: Schedule 20-min schema review call with Nikita before he codes his classifier's insights writer
description:
## Context
SPEC.md §9 Open Question 2: 'Should Nikita be looped in on the schema? Lean:
yes, 20-min call before we mark schema stable.' Now is exactly that moment —
schema is deployed, Nikita hasn't started his Python insights client yet, so
changes are still cheap. Every writer we add after this compounds schema-change
coordination cost.

## Why this is P1
This is time-sensitive in a way the dashboard tickets aren't. Every day Nikita
writes code against his own mental model of the schema is a day of rework risk.

## Agenda to share in advance
1. **Env / model naming.** His classifier should use `env='local-nikita'` (or
   agree a different value) and `model='nikita-v1'` so MODEL_PRICES can be
   pre-populated with zero-price entries and his rows don't flag red in 'Models
   seen'.
2. **`signal_id` convention.** The single most important agreement: Nikita's
   `signal_id` MUST equal clear-pipeline's `signal_id` for the same underlying
   signal. This is the Phase 4 run-comparison join key. If he invents his own
   ids, we lose the Claude-vs-Nikita comparison forever without backfill.
3. **Inference shape.** Does his classifier do one-row-per-inference or does it
   batch? Batching means `input_tokens`/`latency_ms` have different meanings and
   might warrant a `batch_size` column. Decide: support batching now (additive
   column) or enforce one-call-per-inference.
4. **Test signal overlap.** Does his eval set overlap with clear-pipeline's
   inputs? Phase 4 comparison needs shared signals.
5. **Token strategy.** Heads-up that we might move from one shared token to
   per-writer tokens (see separate spike ticket) — is he OK being the second
   user of the shared token short-term?

## Deliverable
Add a short `## Decisions` section at the bottom of [SPEC.md](SPEC.md) capturing
each of the five answers with a date stamp, e.g.:
```
## Decisions
- 2026-05-XX — Nikita's classifier writes as env='local-nikita', model='nikita-v1'.
- 2026-05-XX — signal_id uses the same value as clear-pipeline; Nikita will pull
  signal fixtures from <location>.
- ...
```

## Out of scope
- Actually implementing his insights client (that's Nikita's work in his repo).
- Schema changes, unless the call surfaces something critical.
acceptance_criteria: Calendar invite sent. Agenda shared in advance covering: (1) env/model string conventions for his classifier, (2) signal_id must match clear-pipeline's value for the same signal (join key for Phase 4), (3) whether his classifier emits one row per inference or batches (schema implication), (4) whether he wants to share signal fixtures. Meeting happens. Decisions captured in a short note added to SPEC.md under a new 'Decisions' section.

comments.author: positonic
comments.text:
Observations from building the env-filter (1k2) and cache tile (82h) that are worth surfacing with Nikita during the schema review:

1. **env string convention.** env is a free-form text column. The env-filter strip lists every DISTINCT value. Decide whether Nikita uses one stable env value (e.g. 'local-nikita') across experiments or per-experiment strings (e.g. 'local-nikita-v2-2026-04-25'). One stable value = one checkbox in the filter strip; per-experiment = clutter. I'd recommend one stable env + putting experiment identifier in pipeline_run.name (which is already there).

2. **cost_usd semantics for self-hosted models.** prices.ts has nikita-v1 at {input: 0, output: 0}, so computeCost returns 0 (not null). The dashboard hero then shows Nikita's rows as contributing $0.00 to spend, while unknown models return null and surface in the 'Models seen' table as 'no — add to prices.ts'. Two questions:
   - Is '$0' the right signal, or should self-hosted return null so Nikita's rows don't appear in cost aggregates at all?
   - When Nikita iterates his model to v2/v3, does he need prices.ts updated each time, or should we pattern-match (e.g. any model starting with 'nikita-' → 0)?

3. **cache_read/cache_create meaning for the classifier.** The cache hit ratio tile assumes Anthropic-style prompt caching (input vs cache_read tokens). Nikita's classifier probably has no equivalent. If he sends nulls, the tile ignores him (correct). If he sends zeros, same thing. But if he puts his own notion of 'cached' (e.g. embedding cache hits), it will bleed into the hit ratio and distort the signal. Worth a five-minute alignment.

4. **latency_ms is now surfaced per stage (ticket 7xm).** Nikita's classifier will appear in the latency table alongside Claude. Classifier latency will likely be 10-100x lower than Claude. If we want to compare them, the per-stage table needs a 'by model' split (future). For now, just know that the stage=classify row will be a mix of Claude + nikita-v1 medians.

5. **parse_error semantics for non-Claude writers.** The parse error rate tile (ticket 2id) counts rows with parse_error IS NOT NULL. What does 'parse failure' mean for Nikita's output? If his classifier returns structured output directly (no JSON parse step), parse_error will always be null — that's fine, 0% rate is honest. But if he wraps a JSON contract anyway, this is a useful regression signal to keep populated.

6. **signal_id is the join key for run comparison (Phase 4).** Confirm Nikita will populate signal_id with the exact same value clear-pipeline uses. A mismatch here silently breaks Phase 4 before it's built. Worth double-checking the input data source he's classifying from.

title: Add /live page with last-60-min view and auto-refresh
description:
## Context
When debugging a live pipeline problem, the 30-day aggregates are useless.
Operators need a short-window view that reflects the pipeline as it runs right
now. A separate route keeps the main dashboard un-cluttered.

## Files to touch
- New file `src/app/live/page.tsx` — same structure as
  [src/app/page.tsx](src/app/page.tsx), but passes a minutes-based window to
  queries instead of days. `export const dynamic = "force-dynamic";` and add
  `<meta httpEquiv="refresh" content="30" />` in the page's JSX.
- [src/lib/queries.ts](src/lib/queries.ts) — add a minutes-based variant of each
  aggregate (or refactor existing ones to take `{windowSeconds: number}`). Keep
  the DB filter as `created_at >= now() - interval '$1 seconds'`.

## Design notes
- Meta refresh is the simplest option; no client JS. Next.js with
  `force-dynamic` re-renders on every request, so a 30s refresh re-queries the
  DB every 30s.
- In the hero, "last 24h / prior 24h" becomes "last 60 min / prior 60 min".

## Testing
- Local smoke: insert fresh rows, open /live, wait 30s, confirm the numbers update.
- `npm run build` must pass.

## Out of scope
- WebSocket / SSE streaming — meta refresh is sufficient for Phase 1.
- Tail of most-recent individual calls (that's Phase 3 call browser).
acceptance_criteria: New route /live shows the same sections as / but windowed to the last 60 minutes instead of days. Page refreshes every 30 seconds (meta refresh or server-triggered revalidation). Clearly labelled 'Live — last 60 minutes'. Links back to / at top.

comments.author: positonic
comments.text: Implemented in e82ff0b. New /live route mirrors the home tiles scoped to 3600s with a meta httpEquiv refresh of 30s. Hero shows 'last 60 min' vs 'prior 60 min'. Env filter works via ?env= the same way as home. Refactored all aggregate fns to take windowSeconds (removing the 'days' parameter), and home now passes days*86400 for historical aggregates but a hardcoded 86400 for the hero so the 24h hero stays correct regardless of the days filter (per SPEC.md). Extracted pivot/summarizeCache/fmt helpers to src/lib/dashboard.ts to avoid duplicating 60 lines across pages. Omitted the stacked $/day bars from /live because a 60-min window has at most 1-2 day buckets so a bar chart isn't informative; added a pulsing live dot and render-time stamp instead. Verified with a seeded row: hero shows $0.01, latency shows 1,200ms, refresh meta tag present. lint + build pass, route map now has /live as ƒ Dynamic.

title: Phase 3a: Grouping quality review UI
description: Build /review/group page so humans can rate clear-pipeline's group-stage LLM decisions (add_to_existing vs create_new). Scope: call-level rating (not cluster-level — defer to Phase 4 run comparison). Uses existing llm_call rows; adds a new call_rating table.

title: clear-api GraphQL client scaffold
description:
Add src/lib/clearApi.ts with a minimal fetch-based GraphQL client.

Env vars (add to .env.example):
- CLEAR_API_URL — e.g. http://localhost:4000/graphql
- CLEAR_API_TOKEN — Bearer sk_live_... service-user key
- CLEAR_API_TEAM_ID — the team the service user is scoped to

Interface:
  export async function clearApiQuery<T>(query: string, variables: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<T>

Behavior:
- POST to CLEAR_API_URL with { query, variables }
- Authorization: Bearer ${CLEAR_API_TOKEN}
- Throw on HTTP errors or GraphQL errors array in the response
- Timeout 30s (AbortSignal)

No code-gen, no graphql-request dep — keep it small.

Add one query: fetchRecentEvents({ sinceIso, limit }). Calls events(teamId: $teamId) { id title description types rank validFrom validTo firstSignalCreatedAt lastSignalCreatedAt populationAffected signals { id source { id name } title description url publishedAt collectedAt } }, filters client-side by validFrom >= sinceIso (clear-api has no server-side date filter yet), takes first $limit. Return type matches the mirror schema shape.
